front: What is the catalytic triad and how does it work in serine proteases?
back: The catalytic triad (Ser-His-Asp) is a charge-relay system: 1) His abstracts proton from Ser (nucleophile activated), 2) Ser attacks peptide carbonyl (tetrahedral intermediate), 3) Asp stabilizes His+, 4) Amine leaving group departs (acyl-enzyme), 5) Water hydrolyzes acyl-enzyme (product release).
